bucket-troll.overview: The Bucket Troll is a medium-size brawler with 18 HP and 3 action tokens. It throws a big rock at range 12 or swings its Meaty Fists in melee, and its signature ability lets it hurl a nearby small monster or Small Obstacle across the battlefield as a weapon.
bucket-troll.role: With solid HP and a d8 melee swing carrying a hit bonus, the Bucket Troll is suited to front-line scrapping, while Me Throw Big Rock gives it a 12-inch reach to threaten from afar. Its defining trick, Me Throw Monster, costs one energy to fling a small monster or Small Obstacle 8+d6 inches over low terrain, damaging the projectile and any enemies it lands against, making it adept at repositioning and collateral disruption. The optional brace clause halves a thrown ally's damage. It excels when small bodies or obstacles sit nearby and struggles to leverage the toss without them.

cultist-bat.overview: The Cultist Bat is a small (S), 10-point support melee monster with 3 action tokens, movement 6, EmI 7, and 11 HP. It bites with three siphoning d6 swings and grants Blood Boost Tokens that let allies amplify their melee attacks at the cost of friendly HP.
cultist-bat.role: It functions as a fast, fragile melee enabler. With only 11 HP and a flat d6 defense (no bonus), it is suited to staying mobile via its movement of 6 rather than trading blows directly. Its Bite delivers three siphon swings, while Blood Boost converts a friendly monster's HP into tokens that add d6 per token to an ally's melee roll, with an all-or-nothing re-roll. This rewards pairing it with hard-hitting melee allies who can absorb the HP loss, but it struggles when isolated or low on healthy friendly targets.

doctor-speeding-ticket.overview: Doctor Speeding Ticket is a Medium 11-party-point support monster with 3 action tokens, 12 HP, and a d6 defense die. Its kit pairs healing with battlefield control: its melee Doctor Punch heals an ally on dealing damage, while a passive aura stops nearby enemies from starting Move or Charge actions.
doctor-speeding-ticket.role: It functions as a healer-controller. The Doctor ability restores d10 HP to a monster within 6", and Doctor Punch heals an ally within 3" for d3 whenever it damages an enemy, so it sustains allies even while attacking. Its Speeding Ticket passive locks down enemies within 2", preventing them from starting Move or Charge actions, making it suited to anchoring chokepoints and pinning melee threats. With modest 12 HP, movement 5, and an unmodified d6 defense, it is fragile in prolonged exchanges and struggles when enemies Charge it directly, since that exception suspends the aura.

gnorc-big-bomber.overview: The Gnorc Big Bomber is a Small, fragile 10-point monster whose kit revolves around planting Big Bomb tokens that detonate for randomized area damage. Its melee Bomb Bonk swings twice with a high critical bonus, but its defining tool is the volatile bomb that can be kicked or struck across the battlefield.
gnorc-big-bomber.role: The Gnorc Big Bomber is a zone-control and area-damage specialist that excels at threatening clustered enemies through its Big Bomb token, which counts as a Small Obstacle and explodes on a d6 roll of 5+ each turn, dealing d20 damage within 3 inches and up to 6 within 6. With only 9 HP and a flat d6 defense, it struggles in sustained exchanges and is suited to placing bombs at range, then retreating. Its twin-swing Bomb Bonk with a large crit bonus offers a melee threat, but the single-bomb limit and randomized detonation make it inconsistent and timing-dependent.

gnorc-big-drummer.overview: The Gnorc Big Drummer is a small support monster built around a passive aura: friendly Gnorcs within 9 inches add +2 to all attack dice rolls, while nearby friendly non-Gnorc small monsters add +1. Its sole attack, Bang! Bang! Bang!, makes two melee swings.
gnorc-big-drummer.role: The Big Drummer is a fragile, low-cost buffer whose value comes from positioning rather than combat, carrying only 9 HP and a d6 defense. Its passive aura boosts the attack rolls of friendly Gnorcs and small monsters within 9 inches, and for 2 energy and an action it can extend a +2 bonus to all friendly Gnorc and Small monsters for the round. It excels at amplifying a swarm of low-cost allies and is suited to massed forces, with stacking multiple drummers giving diminishing but additive returns. It struggles when isolated or focused down, since its modest melee and defenses offer little independence.

gnorc-pillager.overview: The **Gnorc Pillager** is a small, fragile melee swarm unit with low durability (6 HP, d6 defense) and a two-swing **Gnorc Bonk** melee attack. Its kit revolves around appearing in numbers: it pulls in nearby allied Gnorcs, reflexively charges encroaching enemies, and can respawn after death.
gnorc-pillager.role: The Gnorc Pillager is a cheap (6 party points), low-HP melee skirmisher built to operate as a pack. *Rarely Alone* lets a second Gnorc free-Move after one activates, so it excels when fielded in multiples and clustered for coverage. *Territorial, Brazen Idiots* forces it to charge and Basic Attack enemies ending movement within 6", giving strong reactive zone control but exposing the fragile unit, mitigated only by an emotional-instability test in grave danger. *A Distant Snorting From Beyond The Hills* can replace a destroyed Gnorc on a 4+, supporting attrition. It is suited to swarming and area denial, but struggles when isolated.

head-stone.overview: Head Stone is a medium-sized monster costing 14 party points, with 3 action tokens, 3 movement, 5 EmI, and 14 HP. It attacks at melee range with Big Chomp and centers on the *Rise in Peace* ability, which lets it deploy onto the battlefield late from reserve.
head-stone.role: Head Stone is built around its *Rise in Peace* ability and the Late Arrival rule, which lets it stay off the table at the start of the game and then enter via Energy: spending 2 Energy to set up anywhere more than 1 inch from enemies and immediately activate, or 1 Energy to appear in base contact with any monster as it is destroyed. This makes it suited to ambushing and reacting to losses, threatening with its single-swing melee Big Chomp once it arrives. With moderate HP, movement, and a solid d8 defense, it excels at opportunistic placement but offers little ranged reach.

mimic.overview: The Mimic is a Large ambush predator built around disguise and devouring prey. It cannot move at all, but teleports across the battlefield, biting with its d12 Chomp and dragging foes in with a ranged, yanking Tongue Lash, healing itself whenever it finishes an enemy with a basic attack.
mimic.role: With 0 movement and a passive forbidding Move actions, the Mimic relies entirely on its **Has This Always Been Here???** repositioning, which is free when lurking near a board edge with no enemies within 6" but costs energy and bars use within 1" of foes, suiting it to patient ambushes over chases. Its Tongue Lash yanks distant targets into Chomp range, and Insatiable Horror restores d6 HP (potentially above its maximum) on basic-attack kills, so it excels at isolating and finishing weakened prey but struggles to relocate once pinned in melee.

mr-devil.overview: Mr. Devil is a Large monster built around negotiation. Through his briefcase of deals he can heal, hand out Action Tokens, grant energy, or teleport monsters, and his **Let's Make a Deal** Free Action lets him broker an exchange with the opponent. His melee strike, An Offer They Can't Refuse, hits hard on a critical.
mr-devil.role: With 30 HP, a d8 defense, and only one single-swing melee attack, Mr. Devil is suited to a manipulation and support role rather than sustained brawling, though his attack's high to-hit and crit bonuses make occasional strikes punishing. His kit revolves around the three briefcase deals: free-action **Let's Make a Deal** offers a mutual trade, while the energy-cost **An Offer They Can't Refuse** forces an adjacent enemy through an Emotional Instability test, extracting a deal on a failure or triggering a free Basic Attack on a success. He excels at bending tempo, healing, and positioning, but is limited by the one-deal-per-player-per-turn cap and reliance on opponent cooperation.

mushroom-farmer.overview: The Mushroom Farmer is a medium-sized monster with a 20-point party cost, 4 action tokens, 5 movement, an EmI of 6, and 20 HP behind a d6 defense die. Its attacks and special abilities are not yet finalized in the source sheet.
mushroom-farmer.role: Because its attacks, abilities, and keywords remain unwritten in the current source sheet (v1.4, marked work in progress), the Mushroom Farmer's battlefield role cannot yet be defined. Its baseline numbers point to a mid-cost, medium-frame unit with average mobility, moderate durability from 20 HP and a defensive die with bonuses, and a generous pool of action tokens, but how those resources are spent depends on a kit that is still being finalized.

mushroom-scamp.overview: The Mushroom Scamp is a Small monster costing 4 party points, with 2 action tokens, 5 movement, an EmI of 6, and only 3 HP, defended by a d6 die (no bonus, +2 on crits). Its attacks and abilities have not yet been written.
mushroom-scamp.role: This monster's rules are still being finalized: its source sheet (v1.4) lists no basic attacks, special abilities, or keywords, so its battlefield role cannot yet be described. The defensive profile available so far is fragile, with just 3 HP behind a flat d6 defense that gains a small bonus only on critical rolls. Until its kit is written, any tactical assessment would be speculative.

penguin-rogue.overview: The Penguin Rogue is a Small, 10-party-point skirmisher with 3 action tokens, high mobility (6 movement, 6 EmI), and only 10 HP. It fights with a two-swing ranged Throwing Knife and a four-swing repositioning Dagger, relying on teleportation and evasion to survive.
penguin-rogue.role: The Penguin Rogue is a fragile, high-tempo harasser whose kit revolves around mobility and avoiding return fire. Its Dagger delivers four swings and repositions the Penguin 3 inches, while the Throwing Knife adds range with two accurate swings; both threaten extra damage on crits. *Shadow Waddle* lets it teleport for an Energy as a Move, either escaping deep into safety or isolating a lone enemy for a strike. *Nimble Feathered Dodging Machine* reactively boosts Defense and grants a free Move when targeted. It excels at picking off exposed foes and slipping away, but its low HP makes it suited to evasion over standing fights.

schnoz.overview: Schnoz is a Small, 5-party-point support monster with 4 HP and a single weak melee attack, Attempt To Eat. Its kit centers on Sniff For Snacks, a free d8 roll that forages random benefits, most often healing allies or handing out extra Action Tokens.
schnoz.role: Schnoz is a fragile, utility-focused supporter: with only 4 HP, a d6 defense, and one swing of Attempt To Eat, it contributes little in direct combat and struggles when forced to trade blows. Its value comes from Sniff For Snacks, a no-energy d8 gamble that can heal a friendly monster within 6", grant Action Tokens and Energy, or, on an 8, chain into an extra activation. The random chart, including a chance to self-damage, makes it suited to staying near allies and amplifying their actions rather than acting alone.

small-head-stone.overview: Small Head Stone is a small monster costing 5 party points, with 3 action tokens, 3 movement, an EmI of 5, and 6 HP. It defends with a d6 plus 2 (and a +2 crit bonus) and makes a single melee Chomp attack. Its Rise in Peace ability lets it deploy onto the table mid-game from reserve.
small-head-stone.role: Small Head Stone is a flexible, low-cost skirmisher built around delayed, opportunistic deployment. Its single d6 Chomp and modest defenses suggest it is not a frontline brawler, but Rise in Peace defines its kit: it can be held off-table with Late Arrival, then spend Energy to set up anywhere more than 1 inch from enemies and immediately activate, or to appear in base contact with a monster the moment one is destroyed. This makes it suited to seizing objectives, ambushing, and reacting to the board state, while it struggles in straight attrition against tougher attackers.

small-tumble-stone.overview: The Small Tumble Stone is a small monster costing 5 party points with 6 HP and a d6+2 defense (with a +2 crit bonus). It fields a single melee Chomp attack and a friend-assisted Tumbling Tumble Stone ability that lets a nearby ally launch it across the battlefield as a damaging projectile.
small-tumble-stone.role: With 3 action tokens, 3 movement, and only one modest melee Chomp, the Small Tumble Stone is suited to disruption rather than sustained dueling. Its kit centers on Tumbling Tumble Stone: for 1 energy and 1 action, a friendly monster within 1 inch can hurl it 8+d6 inches in a straight line, dealing d3 damage to every model its base collides with until it strikes an enemy or impassable terrain. This makes it excel as a team-dependent battering piece, rewarding tight ally positioning, while its 6 HP and lack of independent rush options leave it reliant on friends and vulnerable when isolated.

snapping-turtle-knight.overview: The Snapping Turtle Knight is a medium, hard-to-kill melee defender that can bond to a friendly monster at deployment, redirecting enemy melee Basic Attacks aimed at its ward onto itself for Energy. Its Hurts to Touch reaction also deals d3 damage to any monster striking it with a melee Basic Attack.
snapping-turtle-knight.role: It is built as a bodyguard. With 18 HP, the hard-to-kill keyword, and a d8 defense die boosted by a +4 crit bonus, it is suited to absorbing punishment, while Fealty of The Turtle lets it intercept enemy melee Basic Attacks against a bonded ally within 2" by spending Energy. Hurts to Touch punishes melee attackers with d3 damage, so it excels at baiting and tanking melee. Losing its bonded monster strips hard-to-kill but adds Emotional Instability and +2 to its attacks. Its single d8 melee swing and lack of ranged options leave it struggling to project offense.

tumble-stone.overview: Tumble Stone is a medium-sized monster (14 party points, 14 HP) with a single melee bite, Big Chomp. Its defining ability lets a nearby friendly monster push it 8"+d6" in a straight line, turning it into a living bowling ball that damages and scatters enemies it crashes into.
tumble-stone.role: Tumble Stone is a momentum-based disruptor that depends on teamwork: with three action tokens and a d8+2 defense it is durable but slow on its own, and its signature push requires a friendly monster within 1". When launched, it rolls 8"+d6", dealing d6 (or d6+2 on a 4+) to every enemy its base collides with, and on a 4+ ignores all terrain and models to plow a clear path. It excels at long-range charges that strike multiple foes and can scatter small or medium enemies 3", risking falling damage. It struggles to act independently without an ally to set it rolling.

walrus-champion.overview: The Walrus Champion is a medium-sized, 20-point melee bruiser with 25 HP, 3 action tokens, and a two-swing punch. Its passive, #1 World Champion, awards Championship Belt Tokens for damaging enemies, each granting a stacking bonus to its attack rolls.
walrus-champion.role: The Walrus Champion is a front-line melee fighter built around momentum. Its World Champion Punch swings twice with a solid bonus and high crit bonus, while #1 World Champion grants a Championship Belt Token each turn it first damages an enemy with a Basic Attack, adding +3 to all Attack Dice per token up to five tokens. It therefore excels once it lands repeated hits across multiple foes, snowballing its accuracy and damage, and is suited to sustained engagements. With average movement and no defensive bonus beyond a strong crit defense, it struggles when kept at range or focused down before its tokens accumulate.

wimpy-guard.overview: The Wimpy Guard is a small (S), 10-party-point melee bodyguard with 3 action tokens, 6" movement, EmI 8, and 12 HP. It wields a single executioner-tagged melee swing and leans on reaction abilities to draw enemy attacks onto itself and convert incoming harm into extra actions.
wimpy-guard.role: The Wimpy Guard is suited to a protective, reactive frontline role. *Cowardly Martyr* lets it attempt an Emotional Instability test (2d6 against its EmI of 8) to reposition and redirect a Basic Attack aimed at a nearby ally onto itself, adding +2 Defense for the turn — so it can shield friendly models within 6", though the gating test succeeds only a little under half the time. It soaks the redirected hit with its 12 HP and d6+2 defense, while *Strengthened By Trauma* grants an Action Token whenever a friendly model within 9" takes Basic Attack or Special Ability damage, fueling its 3-action economy. Its lone d10 executioner swing (+3, massive crit bonus) makes it a situational threat rather than a sustained attacker.
